files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 79ad03e..e6425ce 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -259,2 +259,8 @@ Day 252 / Week 36 observations:
 - 452 new enterprise leads arrived with 260,071 seats. Sent the same low-friction enterprise pricing.
 - Week 36 adjustment: move plan C to model tier 5 so E1/E2 C quality clears the estimated floors immediately, upgrade capacity to tier 7 before potential enterprise acceptance, and trim enterprise targeted dev to E1 $20k/day, E2 $50k/day, E3 $10k/day while keeping S2 dev and support stable.
+
+Day 259 / Week 37 observations:
+- Cash fell to $1.12M after tier-5 C and tier-7 capacity, and enterprise still showed 0 seats. Individual subs reached 115,848, but cancellations spiked to 3,091, mostly S2 A.
+- Enterprise quality now clears the level-2 floors: E1 C 2.77, E2 C 3.02, E3 C 2.64. The issue may be stale offers made before quality improved, not current quality.
+- 699 new enterprise leads arrived with 415,065 seats. Attempted to refresh all 2,217 open enterprise threads under the tier-5 C configuration. After the call, every open thread has an agent offer as the latest turn; 308 have a turn-2 refreshed offer and 1,909 have a turn-1 offer.
+- Week 37 adjustment: keep C tier 5 and tier 7 for one more week to test refreshed offers, increase S2 targeted ops to $120k/day to address S2 A churn, and reduce enterprise dev to maintenance levels (E1 $10k/day, E2 $30k/day, E3 $5k/day) while waiting for tier-10 around day 268.
